ChatNow is how Lilly talks to AI. Every day, tens of thousands of employees open ChatNow to get work done — asking questions, running analyses, invoking specialized agents. It's the front door to Cortex, Lilly's enterprise AI platform. And it needs to be fast, intuitive, and relentlessly reliable. We're looking for a Senior Staff Software Engineer to own the technical direction and delivery of ChatNow's core platform: the routing engine that matches intent to the right agent, the integration layer that connects a growing ecosystem of AI capabilities, and the experience layer that makes it all feel effortless. You'll set the technical bar for one of Lilly's most visible AI products — and ship code that the entire company uses every day.
[...]
What You'll DoYou'll define the architecture and drive implementation of ChatNow's conversation routing engine — the system that takes a user's intent and connects it to the right AI agent, model, or workflow.
[...]
You'll own the agent integration layer, designing how ChatNow connects to Lilly's expanding network of specialized AI agents on Cortex. You'll build streaming chat interfaces in React and TypeScript, backed by scalable services that handle thousands of concurrent sessions with low latency across multiple AI providers.
[...]
You'll own features end to end. Design, build, test, deploy, monitor. No hand-offs. You'll operate ChatNow on Cortex and Hangar — Lilly's enterprise Kubernetes platform — instrumented with LGTM observability so you can see exactly how routing, latency, and agent performance behave in production.
[...]
You'll integrate LLM capabilities through Cortex's model gateway, supporting multi-model routing with fallback and load balancing. You'll implement MCP integrations that connect agents to enterprise data sources and tools. And you'll use agentic coding tools like Claude Code and GitHub Copilot as a daily force multiplier — not a novelty.

This is AI-native engineering at staff level. You'll shape how agents are registered, discovered, and invoked on Cortex — defining the framework that the rest of the organization builds on. The Cardiometabolic Research (CMR) Therapeutic Area of Eli Lilly and Company, focuses on the discovery of biologic, small molecule and genetic therapeutics for the treatment of cardiometabolic diseases and associated complications.
